Land Rover Defender P525 Engine Codes

mainEntity.name: How much does a Land Rover Defender P525 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned P525 5.0L supercharged V8 engine typically costs £6,000–£10,500 supply only. Fitting adds £1,500–£2,500 depending on your location and workshop. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your Defender.

mainEntity.name: Is the Land Rover Defender P525 engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The AJ133 5.0L supercharged V8 is a proven, long-running JLR engine platform used across the Range Rover Sport SVR, F-Type R, and many other performance models. It's capable of high mileages when properly maintained — but it has known weak points: timing chain tensioner wear, coolant crossover pipe deterioration, and oil consumption. Regular maintenance (5,000–7,500-mile oil changes) is essential.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Land Rover Defender P525?

acceptedAnswer.text: The most frequently reported issues are timing chain tensioner wear (plastic tensioners degrade, causing slack and potential valve-to-piston contact), coolant crossover pipe deterioration (leads to coolant loss and overheating), and oil consumption (some engines burn 1L per 1,000 miles, leading to oil starvation and spun rod bearings if ignored).

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Defender P525 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value. Against a well-kept £70,000–£100,000 Defender, an £8,000 recon is 8–11% of the car's value — definitely worth it. Against a high-mileage £45,000 example, the same recon approaches our 60% Land Rover threshold — get a scrap valuation first.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Defender P525 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. The P525 carries the engine code AJ133 (also referred to as 508PS in JLR parts documentation). It's a 5.0-litre supercharged V8 petrol engine. The 'P525' designation reflects the engine's output in metric horsepower (525 PS).

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the timing chain, or does it need full engine replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — if caught early. A preventative timing chain and tensioner replacement costs £1,200–£2,500. If ignored and the chain skips, timing is lost, valves contact pistons, and you're looking at a £6,000–£10,500 engine replacement. At the first sign of cold-start rattle, book an inspection immediately. Regular oil changes (every 5,000–7,500 miles) are the single most effective preventative measure.

replacementCosts.h2: Land Rover Defender P525 Engine Replacement Costs
replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the AJ133 5.0L supercharged V8. Replacement cost range: £4,500 – £10,500 (parts + labour) based on UK specialist data. The AJ133 has a typical lifespan of 70,000–100,000 miles.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a Defender P525 V8 engine typically takes 16–22 hours at £80–£120/hr (independent) or £140–£180/hr (London/SE dealer).

cta.label: → Compare P525 engine prices from UK specialists

problems.title: Timing Chain Tensioner Wear
problems.symptoms: Rattling noise on cold start, metallic ticking from the engine, engine warning light, possible misfire.
problems.replacementCost: £6,000–£10,500 (if chain skips and valves contact pistons)

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you hear rattle, book an inspection today

problems.recommendation: The AJ133's plastic timing chain tensioners are a known weak point — they degrade over time, allowing chain slack that can lead to skipped timing and catastrophic piston-to-valve contact. The fix: change your oil every 5,000–7,500 miles instead of Land Rover's recommended 15,000-mile intervals. At the first sign of cold-start rattle, get the chain and tensioners inspected immediately.

failureLink.label: Read the full timing chain failure guide

problems.title: Coolant Crossover Pipe Failure
problems.symptoms: Coolant loss with no visible external leak, white exhaust smoke, overheating, sweet smell from the engine bay.
problems.recommendation: The plastic coolant crossover pipes on the AJ133 are notorious for deteriorating every 3–5 years. Land Rover has updated the design, making it significantly more robust, and aftermarket metal coolant lines are now available. If you're experiencing unexplained coolant loss, have the crossover pipes inspected promptly — ignoring it can lead to overheating and engine damage.

failureLink.label: Read the full coolant leak guide

problems.title: Oil Consumption & Starvation
problems.symptoms: Low oil warning light, increased oil consumption (1L per 1,000 miles), ticking from the engine, eventual knocking from bottom end.
problems.replacementCost: £6,000–£10,500 (if oil starvation leads to spun rod bearings)
problems.recommendation: Many AJ133 engines burn 1L of oil per 1,000 miles — not inherently fatal, but if ignored, low oil leads to spun rod bearings and total bottom-end failure. Land Rover issued TSB LTB00485 (2015) acknowledging "excessive oil consumption under spirited driving conditions". Check your oil level regularly — at least every fuel fill — and top up immediately if low.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: Defender P525 — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Preventative — catches the fault before catastrophic engine failure
Straightforward fix; aftermarket metal pipes now available
Regular top-ups; monitor closely to avoid starvation
Specialist repair available; cheaper than full supercharger replacement
Typically requires full engine replacement or complete rebuild

buyingChecks.title: Buying This P525? Check These First
buyIf: * Documented oil change history with 5,000–7,500-mile intervals (not the 15,000-mile Land Rover recommendation)
* Coolant crossover pipes replaced with updated design or aftermarket metal units
* No cold-start rattle from the timing chain tensioners
* Full service history from a JLR specialist with records of oil consumption monitoring
avoidIf: * No proof of regular oil changes — this engine is unforgiving of extended intervals
* Cold-start rattle with no documented timing chain inspection
* Unexplained coolant loss with no crossover pipe replacement history
* Oil warning light or knocking from the bottom end — walk away

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The P525 is a genuine performance icon — a supercharged V8 in a 2.5-ton off-roader. It's the most powerful engine ever fitted to a production Defender, and it's a proven, long-running JLR V8 platform. But it's a performance engine with performance-engine running costs. Timing chains, coolant pipes, and oil consumption are the price of entry. Buy on documented history, not on faith — a well-maintained P525 is a future classic; a neglected one is a £20,000 rebuild waiting to happen.

cta.label: → Compare P525 engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Land Rover Defender P525 (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for rattling or metallic ticking on startup (timing chain tensioner wear warning)
* Service History — including the P525 V8 era (2021–present) and confirmed oil-change intervals — ideally every 5,000–7,500 miles, not the 15,000-mile recommendation
* Coolant Check — Any history of coolant loss or crossover pipe replacement? (known weak point)
* Oil Level — Check the dipstick; AJ133 engines are known to consume oil (1L per 1,000 miles is common)
* Smoke Check — White smoke on startup can indicate coolant ingress from crossover pipes
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any unresolved timing, supercharger, or cooling system issues?
* Supercharger Noise — Any whining, rattling, or unusual noise from the supercharger? (coupler wear warning)
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures (cats, O2 sensors)
